name: AI-Generated Plan Reveal as Activation Moment

fields.definition_origin: Pattern formalized 2023-2024 by Cal AI (Henry Langmack, Zach Yadegari) — used 33-step questionnaire feeding GPT-personalized macro plan reveal at step ~30 → soft paywall. Reached $30M ARR Year 1 (TechCrunch / public founder posts 2024), making it the canonical reference. Mau Baron (Prayer Lock, ~$40K-$150K/mo solo founder, Starter Story playbook 2024-2025) deployed the same architecture for faith vertical: questionnaire → AI-generated personalized prayer reveal → paywall. Distinct predecessors: Headspace's pre-recorded 'personalized recommendation' (rules-based, not generative) and Noom's psychology-driven plan reveal (template-based with light personalization). The novel 2024 mechanic is *generative* LLM output as the reveal's content — not pre-loaded data, not rules-based template selection. Documented in Mobbin teardowns and 2024 RevenueCat / Adapty case studies.
fields.core_mechanism: The AI plan reveal is the engineered Aha moment for long-onboarding apps — manufactured BY the LLM during onboarding, before the user has touched the core feature. Mechanism is a five-step compound: (1) the questionnaire collects 10-30 user-supplied data points (sunk cost + IKEA Effect, item 4); (2) those answers are stitched into an LLM prompt as structured context; (3) a 4-7s theatrical loader (item 13) masks the LLM call latency while narrating the personalization computation ('Analyzing your responses...', 'Cross-referencing 2.4M users like you...', 'Building your plan...'); (4) the LLM response renders as a full-bleed personalized plan card with named plan, 3-5 stat cards revealed in stagger, and brand-color celebration motif (item 6 peak); (5) the paywall lands within 1-3 seconds of the reveal, capturing the user at peak Aha + peak sunk-cost + peak IKEA-endowment. The reveal must reference the user's specific answers verbatim ('Because you said you wanted to lose 10 lb and have 30 minutes a day, your plan is...') — this cross-step stitching is what differentiates the pattern from generic template selection. Aha is created BY the LLM, before any in-app activity; conversion happens on Aha alone. RevenueCat 2025 calls out the trade-off: AI-wrapper trial conversion 5.31% (lower than 10.92% non-AI average) but +14% direct purchase, with net per-install LTV comparable.
fields.neuroscience_correlate: Aha realization moment engages right anterior temporal lobe + medial PFC (Jung-Beeman et al. 2004 'Neural activity when people solve verbal problems with insight'). The reveal's text quoting the user's own answers fires self-referential processing in medial PFC (Northoff & Bermpohl 2004 — strongest reward signal among self-relevant content). Anticipation during the theatrical loader engages ventral striatum (Knutson et al. 2001) — the loader IS the dopamine spike, the reveal is the resolution. Hippocampal binding consolidates the reveal as autobiographical (the user's plan, not a generic plan) — the IKEA Effect's neural substrate.
fields.empirical_evidence: (1) Cal AI public revenue trajectory: $30M ARR Year 1 (2024) on the 33-step → AI macro plan reveal → soft paywall architecture. Fastest-growing wellness app of 2024 (TechCrunch / Forbes coverage). (2) Mau Baron Prayer Lock: extending onboarding from 5 min to 15 min (with AI prayer reveal inserted at end) tripled organic conversion; revenue scaled $40K/mo → $150K/mo (Sept 2024 → 2025) on essentially the same architecture (Starter Story full playbook). (3) RevenueCat State of Subscription Apps 2025: AI-wrapper apps see 5.31% trial conversion (vs 10.92% non-AI average) but +14% direct purchase — net revenue per install comparable. The trade-off pattern is consistent across surveyed AI-wrapper apps. (4) Adapty 2026: onboarding paywall converts 1.35% (highest of any placement); when AI plan reveal is engineered immediately before paywall, the lift is in the upper range. (5) Mobbin teardowns 2024-2025: Cal AI, Headway, Flo, Noom all use cross-step-stitched personalization in the reveal — confirms pattern across multiple successful apps. (6) Counter-evidence: AI plan reveals that don't reference user answers verbatim (generic templates with [NAME] inserted) feel hollow and don't predict retention — the personalization must be felt, not nominal.
fields.measurable_outcome_metric: Aha-event rate (% of installs reaching ai_plan_reveal_completed), Aha-to-paywall conversion (% who complete reveal and view paywall within 60s), trial start rate post-reveal, revenue per install conditional on reveal-completion, LLM response latency p50/p95/p99, theatrical loader duration distribution (target 4-7s), reveal abandonment rate (% who close before paywall — should be <20%).
fields.conversion_lift_benchmark: Cal AI: $30M ARR Y1 — best-in-class for wellness AI-wrapper. Mau Baron Prayer Lock: 3x organic conversion lift on 5min→15min flow with AI reveal inserted; revenue 40K→150K/mo. RevenueCat 2025: AI-wrapper +14% direct purchase lift (compensating lower trial conversion). Adapty 2026: weekly + 3-day-trial paywall (post-AI-reveal positioning) yields $54.50 12mo LTV vs $7.40 without (636% lift).
fields.engineering_tactics: (1) Prompt engineering: pass the user's questionnaire answers as structured JSON context to the LLM with system prompt 'Generate a personalized plan that quotes specific user answers verbatim. Include 3-5 stat cards, plan name, and a 1-paragraph narrative.' Force the LLM to cite at least 3 user inputs by name in the output. (2) Theatrical loader (item 13): 4-7 second animated copy stack ('Analyzing your responses' → 'Cross-referencing 2.4M users like you' → 'Building your plan') with Reanimated 3 fade-stack and progress percentage. Loader < 3s feels fake; > 12s feels broken. (3) Streaming response: use OpenAI/Anthropic streaming API to begin rendering reveal as soon as first tokens arrive (perceived latency drops). (4) Render the reveal as a full-bleed plan card with named plan in 56pt Cabinet Grotesk Bold, 3-5 stat cards revealed in 200ms stagger, brand-color particle burst centered, sensoryFeedback .success on iOS, VibrationEffect.EFFECT_HEAVY_CLICK on Android. (5) Persist plan_id to backend AND to MMKV; show on every subsequent screen as the user's anchor (Investment phase of Hook, item 1). (6) Offline-fallback: cache pre-generated static plan template; if LLM call fails or times out >12s, render the static fallback (with diminished personalization) — never show a network-error screen at this critical moment. (7) Cost management: cache identical-input plan responses for 30 days (sha256 of input dictionary as key) — high cache-hit rate after 1K users. (8) Paywall arrives 1-3s after reveal renders — give the user a beat to register the value before seeing price.
fields.real_world_examples: (1) Cal AI step ~30 — 4-7s loader narrating macro computation → full-bleed reveal of named macro plan ('Your Cal Plan: 1850 calories, 130g protein, optimized for 10 lb loss in 12 weeks') with 3 stat cards (Calories / Protein / Goal Date) → side-by-side annual/monthly paywall. (2) Mau Baron Prayer Lock — 4-7s loader → full-bleed personalized prayer reveal (LLM-generated based on faith tradition, life context, goals) → paywall with annual highlight. The prayer is the user's IKEA artifact. (3) Headway — 5-7s loader → personalized book-summary feed reveal (genre + interest + time-budget cross-stitched) → paywall. (4) Flo period tracker — multi-step questionnaire → personalized cycle-prediction reveal (LLM-augmented in 2024+ versions) → mandatory trial commitment. (5) Noom — psychology questionnaire → personalized weight-loss plan reveal (rules-based + LLM hybrid in newer versions) → trial paywall. (6) Anti-example: an AI-wrapper that shows a pre-recorded video pretending to be personalized — caught in 2024-2025 teardowns; trust collapses. (7) Anti-example: LLM-generated reveal that doesn't quote user answers ('Here's your plan: train hard') — generic template; doesn't feel earned.
fields.mobile_vs_web_diffs: Mobile-primary pattern. The big-button conversational questionnaire pattern + thumb-zone reveal + haptic+audio+visual triple-channel feedback at the reveal moment make mobile the natural habitat. Web equivalent: Typeform-style onboarding + AI reveal landing page + email follow-up that re-references the plan. Mobile push notification on Day 1 referencing the named plan is the natural Aha → Activation bridge unavailable on web (outside email).
fields.platform_design_language_alignment: Strongly aligned with both 2025-2026 platform languages. Apple Liquid Glass (iOS 26+) — translucent material + dynamic morph make the reveal screen feel premium and earned; bolder left-aligned typography is ideal for the named-plan headline. Material 3 Expressive (I/O 2025, 18k-participant study) — its expressive shape system + spring motion + measured 4x faster element recognition are exactly the substrate for the staggered stat-card reveal. Both platforms reward the engineered-peak architecture.
fields.category_fit: Strongest for: wellness/habit (Cal AI, Flo, Noom, Headspace AI, Calm AI), AI-wrapper (Cal AI, Prayer Lock, Headway, Blinkist), faith/lifestyle (Prayer Lock, Hallow), edtech (Headway, Blinkist, Speakly). Acceptable: dating (Hinge prompt-architecture is a soft version), journaling (Day One AI prompts). Weakest / DO NOT USE: pure-utility (calculator), B2B productivity (Linear, Notion users want to start working), social (Instagram, TikTok — no plan to generate), fintech-utility (Acorns — link bank > all). The reveal pattern requires a domain where personalization adds clear felt value; without that, the reveal feels gimmicky.
fields.tooling_options: Vercel AI SDK (streaming responses, framework-agnostic), OpenAI / Anthropic API direct (gpt-4o, claude-sonnet-4 — frontier reasoning required for plan personalization quality), Supabase Edge Functions (server-side LLM proxy with rate-limit + auth), Adapty (post-reveal paywall placement + A/B), Superwall (paywall A/B specifically around reveal), RevenueCat (subscription mgmt), MMKV (client persistence of plan_id), Reanimated 3 + react-native-skia (theatrical loader animation + reveal celebration). For offline fallback: pre-generated static plan template stored in app bundle.
fields.ai_dependency: required: this pattern is fundamentally LLM-dependent. Required model class: GPT-4o / Claude Sonnet 4 or equivalent frontier-reasoning model — small models (gpt-4o-mini, claude-haiku) produce noticeably weaker personalization quality and the reveal feels generic. Latency budget: ~1-3s LLM call + 4-7s theatrical loader = 5-10s perceived total. Cost: ~$0.005-0.02 per reveal (input + output tokens for ~30-input personalized plan). Offline fallback REQUIRED: cached pre-generated static plan template for network-drop case.
fields.claude_design_application: Instruct Claude to wire the reveal as a streaming-LLM + theatrical-loader compound. Example fragment 1: 'Implement the AI plan reveal at step N-1 of onboarding. Architecture: (a) collect questionnaire answers in a state-machine context object (XState v5); (b) on Continue from step N-2, dispatch POST to /onboarding/generate-plan with the answers dictionary; (c) immediately show theatrical loader: Reanimated 3 fade-stack copy (\'Analyzing your responses\' → \'Cross-referencing 2.4M users like you\' → \'Building your plan\'), 4-7s duration, brand-color progress arc; (d) backend uses OpenAI streaming API (gpt-4o, system prompt: \'Generate a personalized plan that quotes the user\'s specific answers verbatim. Output JSON: {plan_name, narrative, stat_cards: [{label, value, accent}]}\'); (e) on response, render full-bleed reveal: plan_name in 56pt Cabinet Grotesk Bold, narrative in 18pt Manrope, 3-5 stat cards revealed in 200ms stagger, custom react-native-skia particle burst (24 particles, brand color, 700ms), sensoryFeedback .success on iOS, VibrationEffect.EFFECT_HEAVY_CLICK on Android; (f) persist plan_id to backend + MMKV. Implement offline fallback: if LLM call fails or >12s, render cached static plan template.' Example fragment 2: 'Audit the LLM prompt: must include the user\'s answers as structured JSON and require the output to reference at least 3 specific user answers verbatim. Forbid generic plan templates. Output a sample reveal showing how user answers are quoted in the narrative.'
fields.stitch_application: Stitch must be told this is a TWO-frame sequence (loader → reveal) and the reveal must show personalized content. Example fragment 1: 'Frame 1 (THEATRICAL LOADER): full-bleed soft brand-color background, centered spinning brand-color arc 80px diameter (Reanimated 3 timing 1200ms loop), copy stack below in 24pt Cabinet Grotesk Medium fading through 3 messages (\'Analyzing your responses\' 1.5s → \'Cross-referencing 2.4M users like you\' 2s → \'Building your plan\' 1.5s). Subtle pulse on background. Forbid blank spinner without copy.' Example fragment 2: 'Frame 2 (PLAN REVEAL): full-bleed warm gradient background, plan name (\'Your Cal Plan\') in 56pt DM Serif Display top-anchored, narrative paragraph in 18pt Manrope quoting 3 user answers verbatim (e.g., \'Because you said you wanted to lose 10 lb and have 30 minutes a day...\'), 3 stat cards revealed in stagger (Calories, Protein, Goal Date — each in different bold accent color #FF6B47, #FFD93D, #6BCB77), custom-illustrated particle motif unique to brand (NOT generic confetti SVG), Continue CTA pinned bottom thumb-zone (\'See your plan\' or \'Start tomorrow\' — NOT \'Buy now\').' Example fragment 3: 'Forbid Inter font, indigo-500 / purple-blue gradient, Lucide sparkle icon. Use Cabinet Grotesk + Manrope + DM Serif Display, brand-color palette explicitly defined.'
fields.ai_codegen_failure_mode: AI codegen defaults to: (1) generic plan template that ignores user answers (kills IKEA Effect, hollow Aha); (2) blank spinner during LLM call instead of theatrical loader (Wasted Loading antipattern); (3) no offline fallback (network drop = no reveal = lost conversion); (4) no streaming response (waits for full LLM completion before rendering — perceived latency doubles); (5) reveal-then-instant-paywall (no beat to register value); (6) Lucide sparkle icon + indigo-500 gradient + Inter font on the reveal (AI-slop tells); (7) no plan_id persistence (refresh = lose plan = sunk-cost destroyed). Counter-prompt: 'The reveal MUST reference the user\'s specific answers verbatim — pass the answer dictionary into the LLM prompt and require at least 3 user answers in the output. Use OpenAI streaming API to render reveal as tokens arrive. Implement theatrical loader (4-7s, Reanimated 3 fade-stack copy) — forbid blank spinners. Implement offline fallback (cached static plan). Persist plan_id to MMKV + server. Forbid Inter, indigo-500, purple-blue gradient, Lucide sparkle. Use Cabinet Grotesk + Manrope, brand-color palette listed at top of file.'
fields.anti_patterns: (1) Generic plan reveal that doesn't quote user answers (hollow Aha, Cal AI / Mau Baron success was on the verbatim-stitching). (2) Blank spinner during LLM call (Wasted Loading antipattern). (3) Loader < 3s (feels fake — too fast for AI to have computed anything). (4) Loader > 12s (feels broken — user assumes app is hung). (5) No offline fallback (network drop kills the most leveraged moment in the app). (6) Reveal stacked under upsell modal (kills peak — DFA risk). (7) Reveal animation too long (>2s) — users perceive sluggishness. (8) Pre-recorded video pretending to be personalized — trust collapses if caught. (9) Lucide sparkle icon + indigo gradient + Inter font (AI-slop tells, see .agent/research/emotional-design/results/AI_Slop_Tells.json). (10) No plan_id persistence — reinstall destroys the IKEA artifact.
fields.regulatory_risk: Medium. (1) EU Digital Fairness Act (Q4 2026 draft) and DSA flag deceptive personalization — the LLM reveal must genuinely use user inputs, not pre-recorded fakery. (2) AI Act (EU, finalized 2024, phased compliance through 2026-2027) requires transparency about AI-generated content; the reveal screen should not deceive users into thinking a human created their plan (a small 'Generated by AI' label or terminology in microcopy mitigates). (3) Apple Guideline 5.1.1(v) data minimization — every questionnaire answer feeding the LLM must tie to declared core function. (4) Apple Guideline 4.0 — design must be polished; failed/broken reveals have been cited. (5) GDPR Article 22 — automated decision-making with legal/significant effects requires explicit consent; a calorie plan probably doesn't trigger this, but a medical/financial plan might. (6) FTC Click-to-Cancel — the post-reveal paywall must mirror cancellation pathway disclosure. (7) For minors (COPPA / GDPR-K / AADC): AI-generated personalized recommendations require verifiable parental consent.
fields.platform_constraints_2026: Apple Guideline 3.1.2 effective 2026-01 — toggle paywalls banned; the post-reveal paywall must use side-by-side plan selection. Apple Guideline 5.1.1(v) data minimization. Apple Privacy Manifest required for analytics + LLM-routing SDKs. Google Play Data Safety requires declaration of AI-content generation. Apple App Review has been increasingly scrutinizing AI-wrapper apps for policy compliance (terms of use of underlying model, output safety). EU AI Act transparency requirements phase-in 2025-2026.
fields.accessibility_implications: Theatrical loader copy stack must be VoiceOver/TalkBack-readable (announce each message change). Reveal must respect prefers-reduced-motion (replace particle burst with crossfade ≤200ms, replace stat-card stagger with simultaneous reveal). Sound on celebration must respect mute switch. Dynamic-type to 200% must not break the plan card layout — avoid fixed-height containers. WCAG 2.2 §1.4.3 contrast 4.5:1 must hold against any background color. Touch target on Continue CTA ≥44×44pt iOS / 48dp Android, also ≥24×24px (WCAG 2.2 §2.5.8). LLM streaming response must announce 'Plan ready' when complete to assistive tech.
fields.pitfalls_caveats: (1) LLM latency variability — p99 latency on GPT-4o can spike to 8-12s; theatrical loader must accommodate via min-duration 4s + max-duration 12s with offline fallback. (2) Cost at scale: $0.005-0.02 per reveal × 100K installs/month = $500-2000/mo; cache aggressively. (3) Hallucination risk: LLM may generate physiologically unsafe plans (calories below 1200, dangerous fasting); add a server-side validator to clamp outputs. (4) Cross-cultural prompt: localize the system prompt, not just translate the UI — register, examples, and statistics must be locale-appropriate. (5) Offline UX: static fallback must still feel personalized (use cached questionnaire answers + template substitution). (6) Streaming UX edge: if LLM begins streaming then drops mid-response, fall back to static; don't show partial broken plan. (7) Privacy: LLM provider receives user's personal answers — disclose in privacy policy, consider on-device models for sensitive data (health, faith). (8) Re-Aha: returning users (reinstall) shouldn't replay the reveal — resume from Day 1+ Activation flow. (9) The reveal must align with product reality: if reveal promises 1850 calories but the actual app is hard to log, trust collapses Day 2.
fields.cross_cultural_validity: Pattern works cross-culturally with proper localization. The system prompt must be localized — translation alone produces stilted reveals. Register varies: US tolerates direct outcome-anchored reveals ('Your plan: lose 10 lb in 12 weeks'); JP/KR prefer subtle competence framing ('A small step every day'); LATAM rewards warmer family/community framing; DACH wants data-grounded justification with statistics. Stat-card content must localize (kg vs lb, kcal display preference). RTL languages need mirrored reveal layout AND the LLM prompt must understand RTL output requirements. Faith/medical reveals must respect cultural sensitivities (kosher/halal/vegan defaults vary by market). Cal AI's success in US doesn't automatically transfer — re-validate per locale.
fields.prescribed_action: Place the AI plan reveal at step N-1 of onboarding, immediately before the paywall: 4-7s theatrical loader (Reanimated 3 fade-stack copy narrating computation) → full-bleed personalized plan card with named plan (56pt display type), narrative quoting 3+ user answers verbatim, 3-5 stat cards revealed in 200ms stagger, brand-color particle burst, haptic+audio+visual triple feedback. Implement offline fallback (cached static plan template) and persist plan_id to MMKV + server. Forbid loader <3s or >12s, forbid generic plans that ignore user answers, forbid Lucide sparkle / indigo-500 / Inter defaults.
fields.questionnaire_relevance: The questionnaire IS the substrate for this reveal. Every question's answer must be passed into the LLM prompt, and the reveal must quote at least 3 answers verbatim — this cross-step stitching is what differentiates the pattern from generic templates. Question sequencing builds toward the reveal: low-friction → identity → goals → painpoint → metrics → reveal. Without sufficient questionnaire labor (10-15 minutes / 15-30 questions), the reveal is hollow.
fields.aha_relevance: This item IS the engineered Aha moment for long-onboarding apps. It is the most leveraged single screen in the entire app — it is what allows long onboarding to outperform short onboarding on conversion. The combination of theatrical loader + LLM-generated personalized reveal + cross-step stitching + brand-color celebration + immediate paywall is the canonical 2024-2026 pattern.
fields.emotional_archetype_alignment: Strongest with Magician (the LLM reveal IS the spell, transformation language). Compatible with Sage (the plan is the curated personalized knowledge artifact), Hero (mastery promise via personalized roadmap), Caregiver (reveal as gentle 'Here's how we'll take care of you'). Conflicts with Outlaw (rule-breaking voice doesn't sit well with structured plans) — adapt the reveal copy to frame the plan as 'rebellion against the default'. Pairs with .agent/research/emotional-design/results/Jungian_Brand_Archetypes.json.
fields.anti_pattern_paired: Wasted Loading Screens (the loader IS the engineered peak, never blank); Hollow Aha (reveal that doesn't quote user answers); Generic Celebration (Lucide sparkle + indigo gradient = AI-slop); Premature Activation (forcing first action before Aha realization); Toggle Paywall (banned 2026-01, post-reveal paywall must use side-by-side); Pre-recorded Fake Personalization (DFA dark pattern).
sources: [Mau Baron Starter Story Playbook — Prayer Lock Architecture](https://www.starterstory.com/mou)
